records.m_Talk_FRA: It's good for wishes to come true, but dying isn't good.

records.m_Talk_DEU: I want to help so that person doesn't die either.

records.m_Talk_ENG: When we return, she'll probably be in the same place again this time.
records.m_Talk_DEU: When we return, she'll probably be in the same place again this time.
records.m_Talk_FRA: When we return, she'll probably be in the same place again this time.

records.m_Talk_ENG: Yeah. As if nothing happened, she'll probably be trimming branches again.
records.m_Talk_DEU: Yeah. As if nothing happened, she'll probably be trimming branches again.
records.m_Talk_FRA: Yeah. As if nothing happened, she'll probably be trimming branches again.

records.m_Talk_ENG: They say there is no death in the Promised Land, yet her death felt... different.

records.m_Talk_DEU: Different?
records.m_Talk_FRA: Different?

records.m_Talk_DEU: You mean there's something different from how we die and come back?
records.m_Talk_FRA: You mean there's something different from how we die and come back?

records.m_Talk_ENG: Whenever the woman who resembles me appears to kill Ms. Audra... the sun is always setting.

records.m_Talk_FRA: Whenever the woman who resembles me appears to kill Ms. Audra... the sun is always setting.

records.m_Talk_FRA: And after witnessing her death, it's morning again.

records.m_Talk_FRA: I can't say for certain, but it seems worth confirming ourselves.

records.m_Talk_DEU: ...Yeah. Thinking back, there's enough evidence.
records.m_Talk_FRA: ...Yeah. Thinking back, there's enough evidence.

records.m_Talk_ENG: If Audra's the one who created the Promised Land, then she must also be the one who designed it to repeat each day.

records.m_Talk_FRA: If Audra's the one who created the Promised Land, then she must also be the one who designed it to repeat each day.

records.m_Talk_ENG: She may have linked the end of the day to her own death. That would explain why the residents of the Promised Land have never seen night.

records.m_Talk_FRA: She may have linked the end of the day to her own death. That would explain why the residents of the Promised Land have never seen night.

records.m_Talk_FRA: Audra is another name for the Promised Land itself.

records.m_Talk_ENG: Could it be that there is a reason night must never come for her?

root.m_Talk_DEU: It's good for wishes to come true, but dying isn't good.
root.m_Talk_FRA: It's good for wishes to come true, but dying isn't good.

root.m_Talk_ENG: I want to help so that person doesn't die either.
root.m_Talk_DEU: I want to help so that person doesn't die either.
root.m_Talk_FRA: I want to help so that person doesn't die either.

root.m_Talk_ENG: When we return, she'll probably be in the same place again this time.
root.m_Talk_DEU: When we return, she'll probably be in the same place again this time.
root.m_Talk_FRA: When we return, she'll probably be in the same place again this time.

root.m_Talk_ENG: Yeah. As if nothing happened, she'll probably be trimming branches again.
root.m_Talk_DEU: Yeah. As if nothing happened, she'll probably be trimming branches again.
root.m_Talk_FRA: Yeah. As if nothing happened, she'll probably be trimming branches again.

root.m_Talk_ENG: They say there is no death in the Promised Land, yet her death felt... different.
root.m_Talk_DEU: They say there is no death in the Promised Land, yet her death felt... different.
root.m_Talk_FRA: They say there is no death in the Promised Land, yet her death felt... different.

root.m_Talk_ENG: Different?
root.m_Talk_DEU: Different?
root.m_Talk_FRA: Different?

root.m_Talk_ENG: You mean there's something different from how we die and come back?
root.m_Talk_DEU: You mean there's something different from how we die and come back?
root.m_Talk_FRA: You mean there's something different from how we die and come back?

root.m_Talk_ENG: Whenever the woman who resembles me appears to kill Ms. Audra... the sun is always setting.
root.m_Talk_DEU: Whenever the woman who resembles me appears to kill Ms. Audra... the sun is always setting.
root.m_Talk_FRA: Whenever the woman who resembles me appears to kill Ms. Audra... the sun is always setting.

root.m_Talk_ENG: And after witnessing her death, it's morning again.
root.m_Talk_DEU: And after witnessing her death, it's morning again.
root.m_Talk_FRA: And after witnessing her death, it's morning again.

root.m_Talk_ENG: So... Audra's death might be connected to the day repeating?
root.m_Talk_DEU: So... Audra's death might be connected to the day repeating?
root.m_Talk_FRA: So... Audra's death might be connected to the day repeating?

root.m_Talk_ENG: I can't say for certain, but it seems worth confirming ourselves.
root.m_Talk_DEU: I can't say for certain, but it seems worth confirming ourselves.
root.m_Talk_FRA: I can't say for certain, but it seems worth confirming ourselves.

root.m_Talk_ENG: ...Yeah. Thinking back, there's enough evidence.
root.m_Talk_DEU: ...Yeah. Thinking back, there's enough evidence.
root.m_Talk_FRA: ...Yeah. Thinking back, there's enough evidence.

root.m_Talk_ENG: If Audra's the one who created the Promised Land, then she must also be the one who designed it to repeat each day.
root.m_Talk_DEU: If Audra's the one who created the Promised Land, then she must also be the one who designed it to repeat each day.
root.m_Talk_FRA: If Audra's the one who created the Promised Land, then she must also be the one who designed it to repeat each day.

root.m_Talk_ENG: She may have linked the end of the day to her own death. That would explain why the residents of the Promised Land have never seen night.
root.m_Talk_DEU: She may have linked the end of the day to her own death. That would explain why the residents of the Promised Land have never seen night.
root.m_Talk_FRA: She may have linked the end of the day to her own death. That would explain why the residents of the Promised Land have never seen night.

root.m_Talk_ENG: Audra is another name for the Promised Land itself.
root.m_Talk_DEU: Audra is another name for the Promised Land itself.
root.m_Talk_FRA: Audra is another name for the Promised Land itself.

root.m_Talk_ENG: Could it be that there is a reason night must never come for her?
root.m_Talk_DEU: Could it be that there is a reason night must never come for her?
root.m_Talk_FRA: Could it be that there is a reason night must never come for her?

root.m_Talk_ENG: Who knows. Maybe she's just terribly afraid of the dark.
root.m_Talk_DEU: Who knows. Maybe she's just terribly afraid of the dark.
root.m_Talk_FRA: Who knows. Maybe she's just terribly afraid of the dark.

root.m_Talk_ENG: Whatever the reason, if we can stop her from dying and end this cycle... I'll try as many times as it takes.
root.m_Talk_DEU: Whatever the reason, if we can stop her from dying and end this cycle... I'll try as many times as it takes.
root.m_Talk_FRA: Whatever the reason, if we can stop her from dying and end this cycle... I'll try as many times as it takes.

root.m_Talk_ENG: There is only one reason outsiders arrive here.
root.m_Talk_DEU: There is only one reason outsiders arrive here.
root.m_Talk_FRA: There is only one reason outsiders arrive here.

root.m_Talk_ENG: Because the Promised Land wished it so.
root.m_Talk_DEU: Because the Promised Land wished it so.
root.m_Talk_FRA: Because the Promised Land wished it so.

root.m_Talk_ENG: ...If we succeed, then maybe we'll be able to leave the Promised Land and return to reality.
root.m_Talk_DEU: ...If we succeed, then maybe we'll be able to leave the Promised Land and return to reality.
root.m_Talk_FRA: ...If we succeed, then maybe we'll be able to leave the Promised Land and return to reality.
